claim.statement: Adjacent touch targets must have sufficient spacing so neighbouring controls are not triggered by mistake. WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 lets undersized targets pass only when a 24px-diameter circle centred on each does not overlap another target's circle; platform guidance recommends roughly 8dp+ of spacing between comfortable targets.

limitations: Spacing requirement interacts with the 24px size rule; dense data tables may need design trade-offs

do: Maintain spacing so 24px circles centred on undersized targets do not overlap
Add visible gaps or dividers between consecutive tappable rows and icon buttons
Increase spacing in error-prone or high-stakes clusters of controls
avoid: Packing icon buttons edge-to-edge in toolbars on touch surfaces
Stacking small tappable links with no vertical separation

strategies: increase target size to clear the circle test

methods: geometric overlap test on bounding boxes
